sourceMovie.title: When Marnie Was There
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit When Marnie Was There for the same reason it broke through in 2014: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Hiromasa Yonebayashi keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Boy and the Heron
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If When Marnie Was There clicked for you, The Boy and the Heron (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and drama, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after When Marnie Was There?
faq.answer: Try The Boy and the Heron, Mirai, Colorful—each shares DNA with When Marnie Was There while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is When Marnie Was There?
faq.answer: When Marnie Was There (2014) is best described as Animation, Drama, Family, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is When Marnie Was There worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
